prompt: Plan a minimal edit to change how Flask applies session cookie attributes such as domain, path, secure, partitioned, and SameSite. Identify the implementation and tests to inspect first.

expected_symbols.name: SessionInterface.get_cookie_path
expected_symbols.why: Determines the cookie path from session and application configuration.

expected_symbols.name: SessionInterface.get_cookie_samesite
expected_symbols.why: Reads the SameSite attribute used when writing the session cookie.

expected_symbols.name: SecureCookieSessionInterface.save_session
expected_symbols.why: Writes or deletes the session cookie on the response.

expected_claims.text: The focused implementation file is src/flask/sessions.py because cookie attributes are read and applied there.
save_session is the final write path for setting or deleting the session cookie.
Cookie attribute helpers should be checked before changing response-writing behavior.
Regression coverage should include session tests and configuration-driven cookie behavior.

forbidden_claims.text: Session cookie attributes are owned by the request dispatch method.
Changing session cookie attributes does not require checking session tests.
